name: Kolkhoz Canonical Poster Palette
notes: Semantic spot-color approximations sampled from broad, relatively flat regions of the canonical poster. Use these as solid design inks; do not sample isolated scan speckles or average them into the colors.

paperCream.role: Primary card field, caption panels, and light negative space

harvestGold.role: Wheat, sunlit crops, warm clothing, and agricultural highlights

fieldGreen.role: Primary fields, leaves, and large green fills

posterRed.role: Primary accent, rank marks, flags, stars, and small focal details

slateCharcoal.role: Sky, workwear, machinery, and secondary dark fills

inkBlack.role: Outlines, typography, deepest shadows, and borders

sunlitYellow.role: Selective highlight above harvest gold; never a replacement for paper cream

deepOlive.role: Green shadows, subdued uniforms, distant vegetation, and mid-dark fills

earthBrown.role: Soil, wood, leather, hair, and warm shadow accents

workwearGray.role: Neutral clothing and machinery where slate charcoal is too dark

usageRules: Build illustrations from flat areas of these colors, then add texture separately.
Keep ink black or slate charcoal around light adjacent fills to preserve card-scale legibility.
Use poster red sparingly as the strongest focal accent.
Do not age colors by mixing everything toward beige or gray; aging belongs in a separate paper or print-texture layer.
For face-card art, use paper cream plus two or three core colors and no more than two support colors.
